Art title 1: When Circumstances Push Against me, May Your Grace Flow Out of Me (by Anthony Gurrola)
Artist Statement 1:
(By Anthony Gurrola) When my children have rough days and keep me awake at all hours,

May the grace of God pull them in closer.

Even when my flesh may want to be left alone.

When work seems to become mundane day after day,

May the grace of God cause me to celebrate the little things.

Even if I am the only one celebrating.

When my mental and physical health starts to tank,

May the grace of God teach me its okay to start new again.

Even when I have to ask for constant reminders of His love.

When my own offenses take a bitter root in me,

May the grace of God be the shears to my soul.

Even when it cuts close to my heart.

When I start to feel the burn out of ministry,

May the grace of God teach me what ministry was all along.

Even if it's only ministering to Him.

No matter what pushes against me,

Whether I am poured out,

Or spilled out.

May it always contain the goodness of God.

If I am handled poorly,

And the contents of my soul are spilled out,

May it be healing to the one who broke me.

If I am handled well,

And the contents of my soul are poured out,

May it be a testimony to the one that healed me.
excerpt:
You Can Remember Before You Can Remember:

There is a thing I've heard from multiple sources which is necessary to do (for expert-level-rememberers, I guess). A lot of our formative memories supposedly happened within our first year (Attachment theory) or 18 months of life. But the tricky thing is those memories don't get stored as concrete visuals or situational details like later memories. They get stored more as feelings and sensations. 

So for some of the most formative memories apparently we will only be able to recall "being warm" or "feeling scared," or "feeling like something red is approaching." As I go back through my memories, I've hit an interesting block. I can't seem to 'break into' memories of the first house I lived in. I can't picture the inside at all, I don't think. Even in old pictures I don't recognize anything. 

So I've started to explore in a different way. I've been trying to invite feelings and sensations more than memories with visuals or circumstances I can describe. We'll see where it gets me.

And as we hold those memories, we can reparent ourselves, as described on previous days. This means speak love to yourself in the ways that you still feel it was lacking.
3dNotes3: Bethsaida: where several of the Jesus' disciples were from, and the location of which Jesus says, "Woe to you, Bethsaida..."
